channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Trump administration aims to cut regulations on US commercial fishing
item.description: Scallop fishing had been banned in the New England waters since 1994 on account of overfishing.

item.title: UK culture minister quits X over ‘abuse and misinformation’
item.description: UK Department of Culture, Media and Sport will also stop using social media platform.

item.title: Violent clashes as Albania’s anti-government protests continue
item.description: Violent clashes as Albania’s anti-government protests continue

item.title: LIVE: Portugal vs Croatia – FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: Live coverage and text updates, with team news, from our commentary stream as Ronaldo's Portugal target the last 16.

item.title: Trump administration renews pressure on International Criminal Court
item.description: The US says it will reject any effort by court to assert authority over citizens, days after judges sue over sanctions.

item.title: Supermarket chaos for air conditioner sale in France amid heatwave
item.description: Supermarket chaos erupted in France as shoppers raced to buy Lidl's discounted air conditioners and fans

item.title: Nine Buddhist monks killed by child driving truck in Thailand
item.description: Nine Buddhist monks were killed after a truck driven by an 11-year-old boy struck a pilgrimage procession in Thailand.

item.title: The last time Iran held a funeral for the Supreme Leader
item.description: An estimated 10 million mourners attended Iran’s first Supreme Leader’s funeral in 1989.

item.title: Fears of new massacre in Sudan’s el-Obeid: What do we know?
item.description: As RSF troops besiege el-Obeid, 500,000 civilians face mass atrocities amid drone strikes and a stalled peace process.

item.title: UK’s likely next PM Andy Burnham faces defence funding gap
item.description: Defence Secretary Dan Jarvis is facing questions over the funding gap and whether Burnham was blindsided.

item.title: ‘Miracle’: Trapped man rescued eight days after Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: Attention has turned to the humanitarian response after at least 2,295 people are confirmed dead, and 13,000 homeless.

item.title: How the North American heatwave could impact the FIFA World Cup
item.description: Which host cities and matches face the biggest risks? What is FIFA doing to protect players and how can fans stay safe?

item.title: Deadly blast at Damascus cafe leaves 6 dead, 22 injured
item.description: At least 6 people were killed and 22 injured after an explosive device detonated in a café in Damascus' Al-Hijaz area.

item.title: Rally for Luxembourg teacher fired over pro-Gaza posts
item.description: Students and supporters of a Luxembourg teacher fired over social media posts held a demonstration for her this week.

item.title: Amid the World Cup, the new pan-Africanism is conditional
item.description: A record number of African nations are at the 2026 World Cup, but the continent no longer backs them all.

item.title: How US-Iran war may push Gulf countries to ‘diversify’ security alliances
item.description: Despite Iranian attacks, several GCC states continue to speak with Tehran in effort to mend ties and forge cooperation.

item.title: What’s behind the deadly bombing of a crowded Damascus cafe?
item.description: The blast near Damascus's main courthouse killed five and wounded 16 as Syria's new state battles to enforce security.

item.title: US wants intervention, but Syria signals diplomacy after FM’s Lebanon trip
item.description: Syrian Foreign Minister al-Shaibani met Hezbollah ally Berri during visit, as Trump calls for Syria to disarm group.

item.title: German prosecutors allege Ukraine ordered Nord Stream pipeline attack
item.description: Former Ukrainian soldier accused of 'war crime' and leading a team of divers to blow up Nord Stream pipelines.

item.title: Spain immigration scheme sees 1.2 million apply for legal status
item.description: Spain immigration scheme sees more than a million applications, with Latin Americans leading the numbers at 67 percent.

item.title: ‘Collateral damage’: 73 Palestinian children Israel shot in the head
item.description: Israel has rejected a UN Commission report that says its army deliberately targets Palestinian children.

item.title: Why is Prada facing pro-Israeli backlash?
item.description: Prada is facing boycott calls after appointing Palestinian singer Saint Levant as a brand ambassador.

item.title: What is the Society of St Pius X? Why Pope Leo excommunicated its members
item.description: The Vatican declares 'schism' in Catholic Church after bishops were ordained without Pope Leo XIV's approval.

item.title: Spain vs Austria LIVE – FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: Live coverage and text updates as Spain play Austria in Los Angeles in the Round of 32 knockout stage.

item.title: Into the Darkness
item.description: Fault Lines investigates allegations of systematic torture of Palestinian prisoners and detainees in Israeli custody.
